team.description: YepCode Team Id (the same at https://cloud.yepcode.io/{team})

put.description: Resumes a previously paused scheduled process. The process will continue executing according to its original schedule configuration.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the scheduled process to resume

put.description: Pauses a currently active scheduled process. The process will stop executing until it is resumed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the scheduled process to pause

put.description: Terminates a running execution immediately. This operation sends a kill signal to stop the execution process and marks it as killed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the execution to terminate

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of team variables. Variables are key-value pairs that can be used across processes and executions.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of variables to retrieve per page

post.description: Creates a new team variable that can be used across processes and executions. Variables can be marked as sensitive to hide their values in logs and UI.

get.description: Retrieves a list of storage objects in the team's storage bucket. Objects can be filtered by prefix to organize files in folders.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: Filter results to include only objects whose names begin with this prefix (useful for folder-like organization)

post.description: Uploads a file to the team's storage bucket. Files can be organized using folder-like paths in the filename parameter.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Object filename (can include slashes for folder-like organization, e.g., 'folder/subfolder/file.txt')

requestBody.description: File content to upload. Can be sent as multipart/form-data or binary data

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of processes with optional filtering by keywords and tags. Processes are executable code units that can be scheduled or triggered manually.

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.description: Search keywords that apply to process name or description (case-insensitive)

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Filter processes by tags (array of tag names)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of processes to retrieve per page

post.description: Creates a new process with source code, configuration, and metadata. The process can be written in JavaScript or Python and includes parameter schemas for form generation.

get.description: Get process versions
Get process version aliases

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the process to schedule (UUID or slug)

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the process to execute asynchronously (UUID or slug)

parameters.name: Yep-Initiated-By
parameters.description: An identifier for the individual initiating the request. This could be an employee ID, a username, or any other unique identifier.

Location.description: URL to check the status of the execution

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the process to execute synchronously (UUID or slug)

parameters.name: Yep-Initiated-By
parameters.description: An identifier for the individual initiating the request. This could be an employee ID, a username, or any other unique identifier.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of script library modules. Modules are reusable code libraries that can be imported and used across different processes.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of modules to retrieve per page

post.description: Creates a new script library module with source code and metadata. Modules can be written in JavaScript or Python and can be imported by processes.

get.description: Get module versions
Get module version aliases

post.description: Creates a new execution using the same parameters and configuration as the specified execution. This is useful for reproducing or retrying failed executions.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the execution to rerun

get.description: Get all service accounts

delete.description: Permanently deletes a team variable. This action cannot be undone and may affect processes that depend on this variable.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the team variable to delete

patch.description: Updates an existing team variable with new value or configuration. Sensitive variables will have their values hidden in logs and UI.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the team variable to update

get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific scheduled process including its configuration, schedule settings, and current status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the scheduled process to retrieve

delete.description: Permanently deletes a scheduled process and cancels all future executions. This action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the scheduled process to delete

patch.description: Updates an existing scheduled process with new configuration, schedule settings, or parameters. All provided fields will replace the existing values.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the scheduled process to update

get.description: Get a process version alias
Retrieves detailed information about a specific process including its configuration, source code, and metadata.

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the process to retrieve (UUID or slug)

delete.description: Permanently deletes a process and all its associated data including executions and schedules. This action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the process to delete (UUID or slug)

patch.description: Updates an existing process with new configuration, source code, or metadata. All fields provided will replace the existing values.

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the process to update (UUID or slug)

get.description: Get a module version alias
Retrieves detailed information about a specific script library module including its source code, metadata, and version information.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the script library module to retrieve

delete.description: Permanently deletes a script library module. This action cannot be undone and may affect processes that import this module.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the script library module to delete

patch.description: Updates an existing script library module with new source code or metadata. All provided fields will replace the existing values.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the script library module to update

get.description: Downloads a file from the team's storage bucket. The file content is returned as binary data with appropriate headers.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a file from the team's storage bucket. This action cannot be undone.

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of scheduled processes with optional filtering by process and keywords. Scheduled processes define when and how often processes should be executed automatically.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of scheduled processes to retrieve per page

parameters.name: processId
parameters.description: Filter scheduled processes by process ID (UUID)

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.description: Keywords filter: applies to process name or schedule comment

get.description: Get a process version
Get a module version
Retrieves a paginated list of process executions with optional filtering by keywords, process, status, and date range. Executions represent individual runs of processes.

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.description: Search keywords that apply to process name or execution comment (case-insensitive)

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter executions by status (RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, KILLED, etc.)

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Filter executions created from this date and time (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Filter executions created until this date and time (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of executions to retrieve per page

get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific execution including its status, parameters, results, and metadata.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the execution to retrieve

get.description: Retrieves the log entries for a specific execution, providing detailed information about the execution process and any output or errors.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the execution to get logs for

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of executions to retrieve per page

key.description: The unique key/name for the team variable. Used to reference the variable in processes and executions.

value.description: The value of the team variable. Can be any string value that will be used by processes.

isSensitive.description: Whether the variable value should be treated as sensitive. Sensitive variables have their values hidden in logs and UI.

id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the team variable

key.description: The key/name of the team variable used to reference it in processes

value.description: The value of the team variable. For sensitive variables, this may be masked in logs and UI

isSensitive.description: Whether the variable value is marked as sensitive. Sensitive variables have their values hidden in logs and UI

createdBy.description: Username of the user who created this team variable

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the team variable was created

name.description: The name of the process. This will be displayed in the UI and used for identification.

slug.description: A unique identifier for the process. Used in URLs and API calls. Must be URL-safe (lowercase, hyphens, no spaces).

description.description: A detailed description of what the process does. This helps users understand the process purpose and functionality.

readme.description: Markdown documentation for the process. This can include usage instructions, examples, and additional context.

programmingLanguage.description: The programming language used for the process source code. Supported languages are JAVASCRIPT and PYTHON.

sourceCode.description: The source code of the process. This is the executable code that will run when the process is executed.

parametersSchema.description: JSON Schema defining the input parameters for the process. This schema is used to generate forms in the UI and validate input data.

example.title: Simple form sample
example.description: This is a simple sample form specification

webhook.description: Webhook configuration for the process. Defines how the process can be triggered via HTTP webhooks.

manifest.description: Process manifest configuration. Contains metadata and configuration for the process deployment and execution.

settings.description: Process settings and configuration options. Includes publication settings, form configurations, and dependencies.

script.description: Legacy script configuration. Use sourceCode and programmingLanguage instead.

tags.description: Tags for categorizing and organizing processes. Used for filtering and grouping in the UI.

example.title: Simple form sample
example.description: This is a simple sample form specification

scopedToProcess.description: Indicates if the dependencies are scoped to this specific process

autoDetect.description: Enables auto dependency detection from source code. If enabled, newly detected dependencies will be added while preserving existing ones.

scopedToProcess.description: Indicates if the dependencies are scoped to this specific process

autoDetect.description: Enables auto dependency detection from source code. If enabled, newly detected dependencies will be added while preserving existing ones.

name.description: The name of the process displayed in the UI

slug.description: URL-safe unique identifier used in API calls and URLs

description.description: Detailed description of what the process does
description.example: A sample process to show how YepCode works

readme.description: Markdown documentation for the process including usage instructions and examples

manifest.description: Process manifest configuration containing metadata and deployment settings

createdBy.description: Username of the user who created this process

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the process was created

updatedBy.description: Username of the user who last updated this process

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the process was last updated

parametersSchema.description: JSON Schema defining the input parameters for the process

programmingLanguage.description: The programming language used for the process source code (JAVASCRIPT or PYTHON)

sourceCode.description: The source code of the process that will be executed

webhook.description: Webhook configuration for triggering the process via HTTP

settings.description: Process settings including publication, forms, and dependencies configuration

tags.description: Tags for categorizing and organizing processes

versionId.description: The version id of the process being aliased

parameters.description: JSON string containing the input parameters for the process execution. Must match the process parameter schema.

tag.description: A version tag or an alias of the version

comment.description: Optional comment or description for this execution. Useful for tracking and debugging purposes.

settings.description: Execution-specific settings and configuration options. Overrides default process settings for this execution.

callbackUrl.description: URL to receive execution results upon completion (success or failure)

cron.description: Cron expression defining when the process should be executed. Uses standard cron syntax (minute hour day month dayOfWeek).

dateTime.description: Specific date and time when the process should be executed. Used for one-time scheduled executions.

allowConcurrentExecutions.description: Whether multiple executions of the same process can run concurrently. If false, new executions will be queued if one is already running.

input.description: Input parameters and settings for the scheduled process execution. Defines what parameters will be passed to the process when it runs.

id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the scheduled process

createdBy.description: Username of the user who created this scheduled process

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the scheduled process was created

updatedBy.description: Username of the user who last updated this scheduled process

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the scheduled process was last updated

processId.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the process that is scheduled

versionId.description: Version tag or alias of the process version to execute

comment.description: Comment or description for this scheduled process

parameters.description: Input parameters that will be passed to the process when it executes

paused.description: Whether the scheduled process is currently paused

type.description: Type of scheduled process (CRON or DATETIME)

cron.description: Cron expression defining when the process should be executed (for CRON type)

dateTime.description: Specific date and time when the process should be executed (for DATETIME type)

settings.description: Settings and configuration for the scheduled process execution

name.description: The name of the script library module. This will be displayed in the UI and used for identification.

programmingLanguage.description: The programming language used for the module source code. Supported languages are JAVASCRIPT and PYTHON.

sourceCode.description: The source code of the script library module. This is the reusable code that can be imported by processes.

script.description: Legacy script configuration. Use sourceCode and programmingLanguage instead.

id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the script library module

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the script library module was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the script library module was last updated

createdBy.description: Username of the user who created this script library module

updatedBy.description: Username of the user who last updated this script library module

name.description: The name of the script library module

programmingLanguage.description: The programming language used for the module source code (JAVASCRIPT or PYTHON)

sourceCode.description: The source code of the script library module that can be imported by processes

versionId.description: The version id of the script library being aliased

value.description: The updated value of the team variable. Can be any string value that will be used by processes.

description.description: The updated detailed description of what the process does. This helps users understand the process purpose and functionality.

readme.description: The updated markdown documentation for the process. This can include usage instructions, examples, and additional context.

sourceCode.description: The updated source code of the process. This is the executable code that will run when the process is executed.

webhook.description: Updated webhook configuration for the process. Defines how the process can be triggered via HTTP webhooks.

settings.description: Updated process settings and configuration options. Includes publication settings, form configurations, and dependencies.

name.description: The name of the script library. Must not have spaces, dashes and dots are allowed

sourceCode.description: The updated source code of the script library module. This is the reusable code that can be imported by processes.

script.description: Legacy script configuration. Use sourceCode instead.

processId.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the process that was executed

scheduledId.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of the scheduled process that triggered this execution (if applicable)

status.description: Current status of the execution (RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, KILLED, etc.)

timeline.description: Timeline of execution events showing start, end, and status changes

parameters.description: Input parameters that were passed to the process during execution

createdBy.description: Username of the user who initiated this execution

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the execution was started

comment.description: Optional comment or description for this execution

returnValue.description: The return value from the process execution (if completed successfully)

settings.description: Execution-specific settings and configuration used for this run

agentPoolSlug.description: Agent pool where the execution was executed
